title: Heleochloa pygmaea
taxonomy: Heleochloa pygmaea is a species of grass, belonging to the family Cyperaceae. It is a small, annual grass native to Australia. The classification of Heleochloa pygmaea is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, and then into the phylum Magnoliophyta. Within the family Cyperaceae, Heleochloa pygmaea is placed in the subfamily Cyperoideae. This classification helps to understand the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of Heleochloa pygmaea.
